summary: The current-HEAD test plan covers the guarded closure happy path, typed-stop/resume behavior, repair-driven re-review and HEAD rebinding, explicit external-authority boundaries, and a non-regressing conformance baseline.
inspection_summary: At HEAD 66131d96a869fc317519a66c13ea0e3b0b8d3e1d, compared OCR-S-1..8 and OCR-T-1..5 with the unit, independent-review, and production-shaped E2E cases; inspected current verification TAP/JSON evidence and the origin/main conformance baseline. Re-ran 37 relevant tests: 37 passed, 0 failed.
judgment_delta: Initial concern: only a unit-composed happy path might be covered. Final conclusion: current E2E S-001 proves the public CLI's production owner path through managed-worktree commit, separate read-only review, needs_changes repair, verification/re-review, and final_prepare; S-002 and unit cases cover the typed producer/resume matrix.

Initial concern: head changes or external effects could bypass the intended guard. Final conclusion: unit and E2E cases assert repair HEAD advance plus verify/review replay, current-HEAD final_prepare gating, and rejection of PR-create, merge, critical-waiver, deploy, publish, and generic material-effect seams before invocation.

Initial concern: validation could hide an architecture regression. Final conclusion: current conformance remains within the fetched origin/main baseline and improves it from 81 violations (66 undeclared, 5 budget, 10 orphan) to 80 (66, 5, 9); no category regresses.
